hours_formula_detail: Louisiana applies the flat federal ABAWD work standard: at least 80 hours per month (averaging 20 hours/week). General, self-initiated volunteer / unpaid community-service work counts toward those 80 hours at ACTUAL measured hours — it is NOT an allotment-divided-by-minimum-wage workfare formula for general volunteering. Primary LDH/DCFS sources state the requirement verbatim: 'You must spend at least 80 hours each month doing one or a combination of the following activities: Working, Volunteering, or Participating in job training or similar activities' (LDH ABAWD page), and 'Work in a job (for pay or as a volunteer) for at least 80 hours per month' (DCFS news/1076). NOTE on workfare: standard FNS guidance language surfaced for Louisiana lists 'Participate in workfare for the number of hours assigned to you each month (the number of hours will depend on the amount of your SNAP benefit)' as a SEPARATE, optionally-assigned program path — that allotment/benefit-based hours figure applies only to an assigned workfare placement, NOT to general self-initiated nonprofit volunteering, which counts at actual hours toward the flat 80. Therefore the general-volunteer-lane classification is federal_80. OBBBA/HR1 (P.L. 119-21, signed July 4, 2025) did not change the 80-hour figure; it expanded the population subject to the requirement (age 18-64, dependent exemption only if youngest child under 14, removal of certain exemptions) and curtailed area waivers.
minimum_wage_detail: Louisiana has NO state minimum-wage statute (one of the handful of states without one), so the federal floor of $7.25/hr applies in any context that references minimum wage (e.g., an assigned workfare placement). However, minimum wage is NOT used in the general-volunteer-lane hours computation because Louisiana uses the flat 80-hour standard, not an allotment/minimum-wage formula for general community service. Per instructions, minimumWageUsd is set to 0 for a federal_80 classification. (Pending legislation noted but not enacted: HB 353 — $12->$15 — was rejected 4/9/2026; SB 230 — $10.25 effective 1/1/2027 — was still pending as of mid-2026 and is NOT in effect for FY2026.)
qualifying_org_definition: Any employer or program offering paid OR unpaid/volunteer work; LDH/DCFS public policy lists 'Working, including paid and unpaid work' and 'Volunteering' as qualifying activities with no approved-site list, MOU, 501(c)(3) registration, or provider-registration requirement identified for the general volunteer lane. The operative documentation standard under Chapter 4 policy (C-1130) is 'reasonable documentary evidence.'
qualifyingOrgShort: Any organization offering paid or unpaid/volunteer work; no approved-site list, MOU, 501(c)(3), or provider-registration requirement was found for the general volunteer lane — recipients self-initiate volunteer hours and document them with reasonable evidence.
verification_instrument: No named Louisiana-specific form for volunteer-hour verification. The operative standard under Chapter 4 policy (C-1130) is 'reasonable documentary evidence'; the verification policy explicitly prohibits workers from insisting on one type of verification to the exclusion of other reasonable sources. By analogy with OFS-87 (the named wage-verification letter for PAID employers), the functional instrument for volunteers is a written statement/timesheet from the volunteer organization with hours and supervisor contact info. Recipients are otherwise directed to 'be able to provide proof' and to report via LAHelpU (email/phone). No state-published volunteer-specific form name exists.
accepts_outofstate_detail: No Louisiana policy document (LDH ABAWD page, DCFS news/1076, SNAP 4WR notice, Chapter 4 manual, FY25 E&T State Plan) addresses the geographic location of a qualifying volunteer organization. The operative standard is 'reasonable documentary evidence' of hours (C-1130); any employer providing unpaid work qualifies. The federal default (7 CFR 273.24) does not restrict volunteer-org location; absent any state restriction, out-of-state 501(c)(3)s should be operationally accepted, but no source affirmatively states this — hence 'silent'.
geographic_waivers: None active in FY2026 — fully enforced statewide. Louisiana Act 308 (2024 regular legislative session) prohibits DCFS from seeking or renewing ABAWD time-limit waivers (or offering state-provided exemptions) unless required by federal law. All parish-level waivers (the 33 previously-waived parishes) expired October 1, 2024. No Louisiana entries appear in the FNS ABAWD waivers table for FY2025-2029. OBBBA/HR1 (2025) further curtailed area-waiver eligibility nationwide. The SNAP time-limit period is April 2026 - March 2029.
geographicWaivers: None active in FY2026; statewide enforcement with zero waived parishes. Parish waivers expired Oct 1, 2024; Act 308 (2024) bars DCFS from seeking/renewing waivers unless federally required; no LA entry on the FNS FY2025-2029 waiver table; OBBBA (2025) further restricted area waivers.
submissionMechanism: Recipients self-report and provide proof to LDH/DCFS. Primary channel is phone (1-888-LAHELPU / 1-888-524-3578) or email (LAHelpU@la.gov); documentation may also be submitted at the parish/county office or by mail (LDH/ES Document Processing Center, P.O. Box 260031, Baton Rouge, LA 70826). No named org-facing volunteer-verification form.
reportingCadence: Event-driven / change-triggered, tracked monthly. Recipients self-report compliance proactively and must notify LDH within 10 days if hours drop below 80/month. Hours are counted per calendar month, but there is no fixed monthly submission date document — reporting is triggered by the 80-hr/month status and the 10-day change-reporting rule.
submission_deadline: No formal fixed monthly submission deadline was found. Reporting is event-driven: recipients self-report compliance proactively, and if monthly hours fall below 80 they must report the change within 10 days. Contact via 1-888-LAHELPU (1-888-524-3578) / LAHelpU@la.gov; mail to LDH/ES Document Processing Center, P.O. Box 260031, Baton Rouge, LA 70826.
submissionDeadline: Event-driven, not a fixed monthly date. Self-report compliance proactively; report a drop below 80 hrs/month within 10 days.
conflicts_with_prior: No substantive change to classification, hours, or enforcement — prior file's hours_formula='federal_80', monthly_hours_required=80, enforced=yes, required_prestep=none, and statewide-no-waivers findings are all CONFIRMED for FY2026 under OBBBA.

Surfaced the high-stakes 'classification' field explicitly as 'federal_80' (prior file carried hours_formula but not the normalized 'classification' field). Affirmatively confirmed Louisiana does NOT use an allotment/minimum-wage formula for general self-initiated nonprofit volunteering; the benefit-based hours figure applies only to a separately-ASSIGNED workfare placement.
Added minimumWageUsd=0 with detail noting LA has no state minimum wage (federal $7.25 applies in workfare contexts only); regionalWages=[] (no sub-state regional wages).
submission_mechanism corrected/expanded to include phone and email as the PRIMARY self-report channels (LAHelpU), consistent with LDH/DCFS guidance; prior file emphasized paper/mail.
unresolved: No named Louisiana org-facing verification form for volunteer/community-service hours exists in any readable public source (unlike CA's CF 888). Documentation appears to be a signed letter/timesheet from the organization assessed under the C-1130 'reasonable documentary evidence' standard; the exact caseworker-level instrument is not published.
Remote/virtual volunteering eligibility is not addressed by any Louisiana source — recorded as 'silent'.
Out-of-state 501(c)(3) acceptance is not affirmatively stated; policy is silent and would default to federal rules, but no source confirms.

Whether the FNS 'participate in workfare for assigned hours (depends on your benefit)' language is an actively-offered LA program with the allotment/min-wage formula, or merely boilerplate restating the federal menu, is not separately confirmed from a LA-specific operational document — it does not affect the general-volunteer-lane classification (federal_80) either way.

Exact FY2026 dependent-child exemption threshold (under 14) and full OBBBA exemption changes are sourced to federal HR1/NACo/CRS guidance rather than a LA-specific FY2026 notice; LA's older notices still showed age 18-54 / pre-OBBBA exemptions.
notes:
VERIFICATION PASS (2026-06-22, FY2026/OBBBA): Confirmed the prior HIGH-confidence finding via direct reads of primary LDH/DCFS sources. Classification = federal_80: Louisiana applies the flat 80-hour/month (20 hr/wk) federal ABAWD standard, and general self-initiated volunteer/community-service work counts toward those 80 hours at ACTUAL hours (NOT an allotment/minimum-wage workfare formula for general volunteering). Verbatim confirmations: LDH ABAWD page — 'You must spend at least 80 hours each month doing one or a combination of the following activities: Working, Volunteering, or Participating in job training or similar activities'; DCFS news/1076 — 'Work in a job (for pay or as a volunteer) for at least 80 hours per month.' WORKFARE NUANCE: standard FNS guidance text surfaced for LA lists 'Participate in workfare for the number of hours assigned to you each month (the number of hours will depend on the amount of your SNAP benefit)' as a SEPARATE assigned-program path; that benefit-based hours figure applies only to an assigned workfare placement, not to general self-initiated nonprofit volunteering — so the general-lane classification remains federal_80, not workfare_formula or workfare_if_registered. MINIMUM WAGE: Louisiana has no state minimum-wage law, so federal $7.25 applies in any workfare context; set minimumWageUsd=0 because LA uses the flat 80-hour standard for the general lane. ENFORCEMENT: statewide, no active waivers in FY2026 — Act 308 (2024) bars DCFS from seeking/renewing waivers; the 33 previously-waived parishes' waivers expired Oct 1, 2024; no LA entry on the FNS FY2025-2029 waiver table; OBBBA/HR1 (P.L. 119-21, July 4, 2025) further curtailed area waivers and expanded the subject population (age to 64, dependent exemption only if youngest child under 14). VERIFICATION INSTRUMENT: no named LA volunteer-hour form; C-1130 'reasonable documentary evidence' is the operative standard (OFS-87 is the named instrument for PAID-employer wage verification). REPORTING: self-reported via LAHelpU (phone 1-888-524-3578 / email LAHelpU@la.gov), parish office, or mail; event-driven with a 10-day change-reporting trigger. remote_stance=silent, outOfStateNonprofit=silent, countyVariation=false (state-administered, uniform). Confidence retained at HIGH: primary policy authority directly confirms classification + hours + qualifying-org shape + enforcement, and FY2026/OBBBA sources are fully consistent. Residual opacity is limited to the (un-named) volunteer verification instrument and the silent remote/out-of-state questions, captured in 'unresolved'.

--- PRIOR-PASS HISTORY (preserved from states/LA.json) ---
Louisiana enforces ABAWD statewide (parish waivers expired Oct 2024; SNAP 4WR Rev. 01/26 extends age range to 18-64 and household child threshold to under 14). Public-facing DCFS/LDH policy lists volunteer work as a qualifying ABAWD activity at 80 hrs/month, with no mention of mandatory Employment Development Plans, county assignments, provider registration, or MOU requirements. Multiple prior passes extracted SNAP 4WR Rev. 01/26 and Rev. 11/25, the FY2025 E&T State Plan (~109 pages), the LDH client flyer V6, and Chapter 4 policy manual sections (B-1410..B-1460, C-820, C-840, C-1130), plus OFS-87, OFS 4I, OFS 13SO. Findings: the SNAP 4WR 'ABAWD Work Requirement' section defines qualifying activity as 'Working, including paid and unpaid work' or 'participating in a job program or similar activities we approve' with zero mention of HiRE registration, EDPs, approved site lists, or MOUs; the mandatory HiRE/LWC work registration (SNAP 4RW form) is the GENERAL work-registration requirement under 7 CFR 273.7(a), a concurrent eligibility requirement, NOT a pre-step blocking volunteer hours; SNAP E&T is VOLUNTARY in Louisiana and the state did NOT pledge to serve at-risk ABAWDs; no approved-site list or MOU requirement exists for the general ABAWD volunteer lane. required_prestep=none; hours_reporting=self_reported; verification_instrument=written org statement under the C-1130 'reasonable documentary evidence' standard (OFS-87 analogy for paid work). OFS 18C ('Request for Verification') is used only when an exemption claim is 'questionable,' not as a mandatory pre-step.
